notes: macOS triage tools. Many tools require running with administrative privileges; the operator is responsible for confirming legal authority before doing so. macOS built-ins (sysdiagnose, log show, system_profiler) are referenced directly by the collector script and do not need to be downloaded.

tools.name: AutoMacTC
tools.purpose: Automated macOS triage collection and parsing of common artifacts.
tools.notes: Run on the live host with explicit authority; outputs to a configured directory.

tools.name: Autopsy
tools.purpose: Cross-platform forensic GUI; useful for analyzing collected images on the analyst workstation.

tools.name: mac_apt
tools.purpose: macOS Artifact Parsing Tool — parses live disks, images, and tar collections of macOS artifacts.
tools.notes: Run on the analyst workstation against an acquired image or live mount.

tools.name: Objective-See Tools (KnockKnock, BlockBlock, LuLu, TaskExplorer)
tools.purpose: Read-only persistence and live-process inspection tools.
tools.notes: Use KnockKnock and TaskExplorer for read-only triage; avoid installing real-time agents on a suspect host.

tools.name: osquery
tools.purpose: SQL-based host introspection — useful for read-only triage queries on a live macOS host.
tools.notes: Prefer ad-hoc `osqueryi` queries during triage; do not install the daemon on a suspect host.

tools.name: Plaso
tools.purpose: log2timeline / Plaso — build super-timelines from collected macOS artifacts.

tools.name: Velociraptor (macOS collector)
tools.purpose: Targeted artifact collection on macOS endpoints.
tools.notes: Build offline collector with targeted artifacts; copy resulting binary to USB.
